Details: Based on the results of one study, UV irradiation and ozone can be an effective process for alachlor degradation (85 percent in deionized water). Lower alachlor degradation was seen when experiment was conducted with surface water (60 percent) presumably as a result of oxidant demand and hydroxyl scavengers present in the natural surface water.  It is worth noting, that the use of ferrous ions as a catalyst in the UV/ozone process resulted no increase in alachlor degradation.
